text: Last class you described the clause that limits liability.
Today you say on what condition the contract ends or stays.
Provision
Governing law
Jurisdiction
Terminate
Comply
Waiver
Amend
Enforce
The contract terminates if a party does not pay.
We wait, unless the partner agrees.
We accept the waiver provided it is in writing.
If the partner agrees, we enforce the contract from June.
Thank you for the draft. The provision that terminates the contract if a party does not pay in thirty days is too strict for us.
I see. What would work for you? We can change it, provided both parties still comply with the payment dates.
Sixty days, provided we add a small interest. And the governing law, we prefer Brazilian jurisdiction, unless you have a strong reason.
We can accept Brazilian courts, provided the contract is in English. And any waiver is valid only if it is signed by both parties.
Agreed. We amend the contract in writing, and we will not sign unless every change is clear.
Perfect. If we agree on these conditions, we enforce the contract from June. I will send a clean version by Friday.
We prefer Brazilian jurisdiction unless the partner disagrees.
Each party must comply with the deadline.

Hi Simone, this is Daniel again from the legal team. Thank you for the notes on the clauses last week. The German partner sent the new draft, and I want you to read the conditions before we meet. There is one provision that worries me. It says the contract ends if either party fails to pay within thirty days. I think that is too strict, so I would like a softer condition. Please check the governing law as well. The draft says the contract follows German jurisdiction, but I would prefer Brazilian courts if we can agree. One more thing: there is a waiver clause that lets a party give up a right, and we should only accept it provided it is in writing. If the partner agrees to these points, we can sign. Unless they agree, we wait. Let us talk after you read it. Thank you, and talk soon.

Hello, this is Simone with my notes on the new draft, before our review with the partner. First, the contract terminates if a party does not pay within thirty days, and I agree with Daniel that this is too strict. I will ask for sixty days, provided the partner accepts a small interest. Second, the draft follows German jurisdiction, but I would like Brazilian courts, unless the partner has a strong reason. Third, there is a provision that lets us amend the contract, but only in writing, which is good. Finally, I will not accept the waiver unless it is signed by both parties, because a right that you give up by accident is dangerous. If the partner agrees, we enforce the contract from June. I will raise these points at our meeting on Thursday.

Daniel sends the new draft and asks Simone to read the conditions. Simone reads the provision that ends the contract if a party does not pay in thirty days. She decides to ask for sixty days, provided the partner accepts a small interest. She marks the governing law, because she prefers Brazilian courts unless the partner has a strong reason. At the review meeting, Simone proposes the new conditions to the other party.
